If we consider Theodora bracteata (Aristolochia bracteata) to be a different species, here is a paragraph about it.

Theodora bracteata (Aristolochia bracteata) is a plant species that belongs to the Aristolochiaceae family, commonly known as the Dutchman's pipe or birthwort. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Theodora bracteata include aristolochic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its use is generally restricted due to concerns over potential toxicity and carcinogenicity.
